complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the implementation of the core task data structure into specific subtasks covering: 1) Task model definition with all required properties, 2) Tasks collection model implementation, 3) Validation functions for data integrity, 4) File system operations for reading/writing tasks.json, and 5) Error handling implementation for all operations. For each subtask, include specific technical requirements and validation criteria.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves creating foundational data structures with validation logic and file operations. The cyclomatic complexity is moderate due to validation rules and error handling requirements. The task requires careful design decisions that will impact the entire system.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the CLI foundation implementation into subtasks covering: 1) Commander.js setup with global options (--help, --version, --file, --quiet, --debug, --json), 2) Command structure implementation following the specified pattern, 3) Colorized console output and logging system with configurable levels, and 4) Help documentation and scripts directory structure. Include specific implementation details and testing criteria for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has moderate complexity as it involves setting up the command parsing system with multiple options and a structured command pattern. The implementation is straightforward with Commander.js but requires attention to user experience details like colorized output and help documentation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the implementation of basic task operations into subtasks covering: 1) Task listing with filtering options, 2) Status update functionality (pending/done/deferred), 3) Dependency tracking and validation system, 4) Priority management implementation, 5) Commands for adding, updating, and removing tasks, and 6) Task querying by various properties. Include validation requirements and file update mechanisms for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has higher complexity due to the need to implement multiple operations with validation rules, dependency tracking, and ensuring all operations correctly update the tasks.json file. The cyclomatic complexity will be higher due to numerous decision points in validation logic.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the task file generation system into subtasks covering: 1) Task file template creation following PRD specifications, 2) Generation mechanism for individual task files from tasks.json, 3) Bi-directional synchronization implementation between task files and tasks.json, 4) File naming and organization in the tasks/ directory, and 5) Conflict detection and resolution for manual changes. Include specific algorithms and error handling approaches for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has high complexity due to the bi-directional synchronization requirement, which introduces potential conflicts and edge cases. Detecting and resolving conflicts between manually edited files and the central tasks.json will require sophisticated logic with high cyclomatic complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the AI client implementation into subtasks covering: 1) OpenAI SDK client initialization with configurable base URLs, 2) Authentication handling for both authenticated and unauthenticated endpoints, 3) Environment variable configuration for API keys, base URLs, and models, 4) Client factory pattern implementation for selecting appropriate clients, and 5) Error handling with retries and token usage tracking. Include specific interface definitions and testing scenarios for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves creating a flexible client that works with multiple providers and configurations. The complexity comes from handling different authentication methods, implementing retry logic with exponential backoff, and creating a unified interface across different AI providers.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the PRD parsing system into subtasks covering: 1) PRD file reading functionality, 2) Prompt template implementation for AI-assisted parsing, 3) Response parsing to extract structured task data, 4) Dependency inference algorithm between tasks, 5) Priority assignment logic implementation, and 6) Chunking mechanism for handling large PRDs. Include specific prompt designs and validation approaches for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has high complexity due to the AI-assisted parsing of unstructured PRD text into structured task data. The intelligent dependency inference and priority assignment require sophisticated algorithms. Handling large PRDs through chunking adds additional complexity to maintain context across chunks.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the task expansion implementation into subtasks covering: 1) Subtask generation prompt creation following the appendix template, 2) Subtask creation workflow implementation, 3) Context-aware expansion capabilities considering project state, 4) Parent-child relationship management for tasks and subtasks, and 5) Batch expansion functionality for multiple tasks. Include specific prompt engineering techniques and validation criteria for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves complex AI interactions to generate meaningful subtasks with awareness of the project context. The parent-child relationship management and validation of generated subtasks introduce significant complexity. The context-aware expansion requires sophisticated prompt engineering.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the implementation drift handling system into subtasks covering: 1) Task updating mechanism based on implementation changes, 2) AI-assisted task rewriting with project state awareness, 3) Dependency chain update algorithm when tasks change, 4) Preservation mechanism for completed work during updates, 5) Implementation drift detection comparing actual vs. planned implementation, and 6) Selective update functionality for specific tasks or chains. Include specific algorithms and edge case handling for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has very high complexity due to the need to intelligently update future tasks based on implementation changes while preserving completed work. The dependency chain updates and implementation drift detection require sophisticated algorithms with high cyclomatic complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Batch Operations and Project Initialization
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the batch operations and project initialization into subtasks covering: 1) Multi-task status update implementation, 2) Bulk subtask generation functionality, 3) Advanced task filtering and querying with complex criteria, 4) Project templating system for initialization, and 5) Interactive setup implementation for new projects. Include specific command structures and validation requirements for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves implementing multiple advanced features with moderate individual complexity. The batch operations require careful validation to prevent invalid states, and the project initialization functionality needs to handle various configuration options and templates.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create Cursor AI Integration and Documentation
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the Cursor AI integration and documentation into subtasks covering: 1) Creation of dev_workflow.mdc, cursor_rules.mdc, and self_improve.mdc in the .cursor/rules/ directory, 2) Design and implementation of rule integration documentation, 3) Documentation of task workflows, selection guidelines, and implementation guidance for agents, and 4) Creation of comprehensive README with installation, usage instructions, and troubleshooting guides. Include specific documentation standards and examples for each subtask.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task focuses on documentation and integration rules rather than complex implementation. The complexity comes from ensuring comprehensive coverage of all features and creating clear guidelines for AI agents. The task requires attention to detail but has fewer technical challenges than implementation tasks.
